inputs.label: Parts Cost
inputs.businessContext: Total cost of replacement parts from supplier invoice or catalog.

inputs.label: Labor Hours
inputs.businessContext: Estimated hours of mechanic labor based on job complexity and standard repair times (e.g., Mitchell, Alldata).

inputs.label: Labor Rate per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Shop's hourly labor charge, typically $80-$150 depending on location and specialization.

inputs.label: Shop Supplies Fee
inputs.businessContext: Flat fee for consumables like rags, lubricants, and small hardware.

inputs.label: Hazardous Waste Fee
inputs.businessContext: Disposal fee for oil, batteries, tires, etc., often regulated by EPA.

inputs.label: Sublet Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost of outsourced services like machining, paint, or diagnostics.

inputs.label: Tax Rate
inputs.businessContext: Sales tax percentage applied to parts and supplies (not labor in some states).

inputs.label: Desired Profit Margin
inputs.businessContext: Target profit margin on total cost, typically 25-40% for auto repair shops.

inputs.label: Discount Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Optional discount offered to customer (e.g., loyalty, promotion).

inputs.label: Include Labor in Tax
inputs.businessContext: Some jurisdictions tax labor; check local laws.

thresholds.desiredProfitMargin > 50: Profit margin above 50% may be unrealistic for competitive market.
thresholds.laborHours > 10: High labor hours; consider breaking down into sub-operations.
thresholds.partsCost > 5000: High parts cost; verify with supplier quote.

hiddenLossDrivers: If shopSuppliesFee is too low, actual consumable cost may erode margin.
If laborHours underestimate, overtime or rework reduces profit.
suggestedActions: Verify parts cost with current supplier pricing.
Check labor hours against standard repair times (e.g., Mitchell).
Review shop supplies fee to cover actual usage.
Ensure hazardous waste fee complies with local regulations.

premiumFeatures: Trend analysis of parts and labor costs over time
Detailed profit margin breakdown by job type
